返回本批次

摩根大通在新报告中警告特斯拉股价可能下跌 60%

JPMorgan warns Tesla stock could sink 60% in new note

finance.yahoo.com 作者 freediddy 分数 59 评论 18 2026/04/07 01:44
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 更新于 2026 年 4 月 6 日星期一上午 7:20（MST） 在摩根大通发布的一份新的看跌报告中，分析师警告称，特斯拉 (TSLA) 的股价可能会下跌 60%，这家电动汽车制造商新的年终目标价为 145 美元。雅虎财经高级记者 Ines Ferré 详细分析了摩根大通团队的报告，而 Sevens Report Research 创始人 Tom Essaye 对此预测做出了回应。 00:01 发言者 A 呃，就像我一样，这是今天早上从摩根大通发出的关于特斯拉的野蛮笔记，00:06 发言者 A 你知道，我的 X-feed，我发布了这篇笔记的一部分，我的 X-feed 即将关闭。呃，我只是想告诉人们，这张纸条不是我写的。不要责怪我发表这些评论，但摩根大通再次对特斯拉发出了非常大胆的呼吁。 00:20 Anez 是的，来自分析师 Brian Ryan Brinkman。他说他看到这只股票下跌了 60%，因为他们对特斯拉的目标价是 100 美元和 45 美元。特斯拉目前的股价约为 360 美元。这有点异常，因为市场共识目标价格为 415 美元。但他基本上想说的是，该股被严重高估了。他表示，我们仍然认为 2026 年 12 月 145 日的目标价有 60% 的大幅下跌，并建议投资者高度谨慎地对待特斯拉股票。他谈到了错误的交付估计和未售出的车辆，这给公司的自由现金流带来了压力。而且还谈到，好吧，好吧，特斯拉正在转型。我们知道他们已经转向人形机器人、机器人出租车。但这位分析师正在谈论一个改变的时间表，因为他说这与特斯拉的说法类似，即到 2020 年 4 月，路上将有 100 万辆特斯拉机器人出租车。所以他真的对埃隆·马斯克的时间表提出了质疑。 01:23 发言者 A 汤姆，我知道你想和我一起进入特斯拉股票的沼泽地。您是这家公司的忠实粉丝吗？ 01:30 汤姆 那么，我认为特斯拉的分析在叙述中有点迷失了，对吧？我的意思是，如果我们将特斯拉视为一家汽车公司，那么是的，它的估值被严重高估，很容易下跌 60%。对我来说，如果你真的要看跌特斯拉，那么必须有某种实质性的变化来解释这家公司不再是一家科技公司，而且他们的机器人出租车和机器人之类的东西将会遭遇巨大的失败。我在这份报告中没有看到这一点。所以，在某种程度上，你知道，特斯拉一直是一只高风险、高回报的股票，因为它不是一家汽车公司。汽车公司只是最终让他们乘坐机器人出租车到达他们想去的地方的技术的一部分。因此，我不认为这份报告会改变游戏规则。呃，直到有人站出来说他们在机器人出租车和机器人方面将会失败，这里有一些可信的原因。我认为喜欢它的人会继续喜欢它，他们会逢低买入。

Original Summary

  1. Updated Mon, April 6, 2026 at 7:20 AM MST In a new bearish note out from JPMorgan, analysts are warning that Tesla's (TSLA) stock price could lose 60% of its value, illustrating such with a new $145 year-end price target on the EV maker. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré breaks down the note from the JPMorgan team, while Sevens Report Research founder Tom Essaye reacts to this forecast. 00:01 Speaker A Uh and as I, this was a savage note on uh Tesla here out from JP Morgan this morning and 00:06 Speaker A you know, my my X-feed, I posted part of this note, my X-feed is going off. Uh and I just want to tell people, I did not write this note. Do not blame me for coming out with these comments, but again, a very bold call on Tesla from JP Morgan. 00:20 Anez Yeah, from analyst Brian Ryan Brinkman. And he is saying that he he's seeing a 60% drop in this stock because they have a price target of 100 and $45 on Tesla. Tesla right now is around 360. This is sort of an outlier because you have the street price target consensus of 415. But what he's basically saying is is that the stock is wildly overvalued. He's saying, we continue to see a large 60% downside to our 145 December 2026 price target and advise investors approach Tesla shares with a high degree of caution. He talks about mis-delivery estimates, unsold vehicles, which is putting pressure on free cash flow for the company. And also talking about, okay, fine, Tesla is pivoting. We know that they have pivoted towards humanoid robots, towards robo taxis. But this analyst is talking about an alter timeline because he's saying it's similar to Tesla's saying that there were going to be a million Tesla robo taxis on the road by April 2020. So he's really calling into question uh Elon Musk's timeline here. 01:23 Speaker A Tom, I know you want to get into the uh the swamp with me on all things Tesla stock. Are you a big fan of this company? 01:30 Tom So, I I think that Tesla gets, sort of the analysis on Tesla gets lost a bit in the narrative, right? I mean, if if we're looking at Tesla as a car company, then yes, it's wildly overvalued and it could easily fall 60%. For me, if you're going to get really bearish on Tesla, there has to be some sort of material change to explain how this company is is not a technology company anymore and they are going to epically fail at their robo taxi and and robots and things like that. And and I didn't see that in this report. So, to a point, you know, Tesla's always been a high risk, high return stock because it's not a car company. The car company is just part of the technology that ultimately gets them to to where they want to be on robo taxis. So, I don't see this report as a as a game changer. Uh, until someone comes out and says they are going to fail at robo taxis and robots and here are credible reasons why. I think that the people who love it are going to continue loving it and they'll look to be buying it on dips.

Only 60% ?

anizan 2026/04/07 05:50
特斯拉与现代、丰田、起亚和日产的电动汽车在质量和性能上的差距正在迅速缩小。 特斯拉陷入了边缘创新的困境，不得不做出像特斯拉餐厅这样的噱头，只是为了在新闻周期中保持相关性。
The difference in quality and performance between Tesla and the EVs from Hyundai, Toyota, Kia, and Nissan is closing fast. Tesla is stuck with marginal innovation and has to pull stunts like the Tesla Diner just to stay relevant in the news cycle.

danudey 2026/04/07 06:31
比亚迪正在将其电动汽车引入加拿大，其中车型起价为 2 万美元，比 Model S 低 5.5 万美元：

https://cardog.app/blog/byd-canada-full-lineup

特斯拉仍在营业的唯一原因是美国的保护主义。

BYD is bringing their EVs to Canada, including models starting at $20k, $55k less than the Model S:

https://cardog.app/blog/byd-canada-full-lineup

The only reason Tesla is still in business is American protectionism.

_aavaa_ 2026/04/07 06:45
该文章没有提供任何证据证明哪些型号被发送到加拿大。它只是列出了他们所有的型号。
That article don’t provide any proof for which models are sent to Canada. It just lists all their models.
在 HN 中查看
martythemaniak 2026/04/07 03:23
在长期持有特斯拉股票多年后，我最近用一点虚拟货币购买了一些 TSLQ（2 倍空头 ETF）。我确实认为他们目前的估值已经很成熟了，而且我的想法非常简单：他们的全部估值几乎都基于近乎即时的机器人出租车推出和对人形机器人的近乎垄断，而这些都不会发生。他们尝试过的其他业务都没有奏效，汽车和仓储业务加起来的价值可能是当前估值的 15%。

首先，为什么 Robotaxi 没有出现？嗯，只是没有达到他们需要的规模。除了Waymo之外，还有许多其他公司也在研究无人驾驶系统，并且都取得了良好的进展。 nVidia 拥有基于 VLA 模型的全栈竞争对手，中国制造商也是如此。简而言之，我们将看到来自全球大约十几家公司的强大自动驾驶系统。特斯拉在这方面有一些优势，但现实世界的接受度和扩展速度很慢，对特斯拉的信任度很低，而且他们的大多数汽车在物理上无法运行 L4 系统所需的模型规模。我的 2022 年款 Model Y 的处理能力与我的 Rovers Jetson Orin Nano 大致相同，但那东西不会自动驾驶。

人形部分更糟糕，特斯拉在这方面完全没有结构性优势——他们在制造硬件方面并不比中国公司好，在开发所需模型方面也不比人工智能实验室好。另外，信任度又低。很多人绝对不会让特斯拉机器人进入他们的房子。没有理由认为它们会取得巨大成功，而且类人机器人的实际能力和经济用途还需要几年的时间。

I recently bought some TSLQ (2x short ETF) with a bit of play money after many years of being long on Tesla. I do think they're cooked at the current valuation and my thinking is pretty simple: almost the entirety of their valuation is predicated on near-instantaneous robotaxi rollout and a near monopoly on humanoids and neither of those are going to happen. None of the other businesses they've tried have worked and the car and storage businesses together are worth probably 15% of current valuation.

So first, why is robotaxi not happening? Well, it is just not on the scale they need it to. In addition to Waymo, many other companies are working on driverless systems and they're all making good progress. nVidia has a full stack competitors based on VLA models, as do Chinese manufacturers etc. In short, we'll see capable self driving systems from probably a dozen companies world wide. Tesla has some advantage here, but real world acceptance and scaling are slow, trust in Tesla is low and most of their cars are physically incapable of running the size of models L4 systems will require. My 2022 Model Y has roughly the same processing power as my Rovers Jetson Orin Nano and that thing isn't driving itself.

The humanoid part is even worse, Tesla has absolutely no structural advantages there - they're no better than Chinese companies at making the hardware and they're no better than the AI Labs at developing the models required. Also, again low trust. Tons of people will absolutely not let a tesla bot in their house. There's no reason to think they'll be wildly successful, and the actual capabilities and economic usefulness of humanoids are still some years away.

在 HN 中查看
crypto_throwa 2026/04/07 03:50
谨慎购买 ETF 作为做空股票的一种方式。

ETF 的设计使得在任何时间点，它们大约是 2 倍的空头股票，但它们是通过随着时间的推移购买衍生品来做到这一点的，这意味着股票减少 X 或增加 Y 并不意味着您会在很长一段时间内获得或损失 2 倍或 2 Y。如果股票先上涨，然后下跌，即使最终价格低于您购买时的价格，您也可能会亏损。

我认为做空股票或购买长期看跌期权可能是更好的方法。

https://www.investopedia.com/articles/investing/092815/risks...

这不是财务建议。

Careful buying ETFs as a way to short the stock.

The ETFs are designed such that at any point in time, they are roughly 2x short the stock, but they do this by buying derivatives over time which means that the stock decreasing by X or increasing by Y doesn't mean you'll gain or lose 2X or 2Y over a long time period. If the stock increases first, then decreases, you'll probably lose money even if it ends up lower than when you bought it.

I think just shorting the stock or buying long dated put options is probably a better way to do it.

https://www.investopedia.com/articles/investing/092815/risks...

This is not financial advice.

Buying options and rolling them might cost more than the decay on the etf

lovich 2026/04/07 03:33
人形“机器人”是真的吗？

我看到一些视频似乎显示它们只是由人类远程控制，但我对此研究不够深入，无法确信这是真的。

Were the humanoid “robots” even real?

I saw some videos that seemingly showed them to just be remote controlled by a human, but I don’t research deeply enough on it to be confident that it was true.

在 HN 中查看
martythemaniak 2026/04/07 03:53
这是一个混合体，取决于任务是什么，一切令人印象深刻的都是 Teleop。从抽象的角度来看，这很好——所有公司的所有类人机器人（现在可能有三打）要么是远程操作，在有限的范围内缓慢而谨慎地自主，要么执行例行公事。这就是它们的开发方式 - 使用 teleop 执行一些有用的任务并获取数据、训练模型、在无人监督的情况下执行任务。这一切都是明智的，只是特斯拉需要它以极快的速度发挥作用，以使他们的估值保持在原来的水平，但事实并非如此。它速度慢、成本高，并且需要数年时间才能让数据飞轮正确旋转、让人们习惯它等等。我只是不认为它能按特斯拉需要的时间表工作。
It's a mix, depending on what the task is, everything impressive is teleop. Which is fine in the abstract - all humanoids from all companies (there's probably like 3 dozen now) are either teleop, doing slow and cautious autonomy with limited scope or executing routines. This is simply how they must be developed - do some useful task with teleop and get data, train model, execute task unsupervised. It's all sensible except Tesla needs this to work at warp speed for their valuation to stay where it is, and it just won't. It's slow, expensive, and will take years to get that data flywheel spinning correctly, get people used to it etc. I just don't see it working on the timelines Tesla needs.
在 HN 中查看
danudey 2026/04/07 04:51
对于最终用户而言，“机器人在我的房子周围走动做某事”和“人类远程控制机器人并在我的房子周围走动”之间存在很大差异。这对隐私和安全的影响是巨大的，我无法想象埃隆·马斯克对让这些观点被听到有丝毫兴趣。
There's a big difference to end users between "a robot walking around my house doing something" and "a human remotely controlling a robot and walking around my house". The privacy and safety implications are huge, and I cannot imagine that Elon Musk has the remotest interest in letting those points be heard.

WarmWash 2026/04/07 02:37
是的，但是他们是否考虑到特斯拉机器人将在未来 5 年内取代所有体力劳动，而每个人都可以乘坐机器人出租车？有了人工智能？还有 TeraFab？还有前往火星的轨道卫星上的 3D 数据中心质量驱动器？

看起来像是经典的操纵行为，因此他们可以在我们估值达到 10T 美元之前低价买入

Yeah, but are they factoring in tesla bots replacing all manual labor in the next 5 years while everyone rides robotaxis everywhere? With AI? And a TeraFab? And 3D datacetenter mass drivers on orbiting satellites to mars?

Looks like classic manipulation so they can buy low before we hit $10T valuation
